data.title: Microservices
data.description: WhatsApp employs a microservices architecture to ensure scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services relying on RESTful APIs or gRPC for communication.

data.title: Horizontal Scaling
data.description: WhatsApp uses horizontal scaling to manage increased loads efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Deployment across multiple server instances and data centers.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Data transmitted across WhatsApp services is end-to-end encrypted.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes the Signal Protocol for robust encryption of messages.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses an event-driven architecture to process real-time data streams.
data.technical_spec: Powered by message brokers like Kafka to handle asynchronous processing.

data.title: Global Distribution
data.description: Data is distributed across global servers to ensure availability and low latency.
data.technical_spec: Use of Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) and geo-replication.
